title: The Twentieth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## October 15, 2023 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Twentieth Sunday after Pentecost falls within Ordinary Time in the Christian calendar. The Revised Common Lectionary for this date would feature readings from Job 23:1-9, 16-17, Psalm 22, Hebrews 4:12-16, and Mark 10:17-31 (the story of the rich young man). This Sunday emphasized themes of suffering, faith tested by hardship, and the tension between earthly wealth and spiritual devotion—readings particularly resonant with the biblical focus on justice and material possessions.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating the aftermath of the July 2023 General Convention held in Louisville, which had approved expanded marriage blessings for same-sex couples and had seen significant theological debates. In Colorado, the Diocese of Colorado under Bishop Kym Lucas was continuing its post-pandemic recovery and restructuring efforts. No major diocesan crises or bishop elections were occurring at this specific moment, though the denomination remained internally divided over LGBTQ+ inclusion and scriptural interpretation.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in mid-October with typical early fall weather patterns. The city was focused on the upcoming 2023 election season (municipal elections were in November). The Colorado legislature had recently concluded its 2023 session, and water rights and climate policy remained persistent state concerns. No major weather emergencies or significant Capitol Hill neighborhood disruptions were reported in the immediate week prior to October 15.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The United States was in the early stages of the 2024 presidential campaign season. The Biden administration was managing ongoing Israel-Hamas conflict developments following the October 7, 2023 attacks (one week prior), which had dramatically escalated tensions and dominated American media. House Speaker Kevin McCarthy had been ousted in a motion on October 3, creating ongoing congressional leadership chaos. Autoworkers' strikes against the Big Three automotive manufacturers were ongoing. The economy and inflation remained central political concerns heading into fall 2023.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Israel-Hamas war had just begun on October 7, 2023, with massive Hamas attacks followed by Israeli military response and humanitarian crisis development in Gaza. Ukraine continued its ongoing conflict with Russia, now in its second year. Global economic uncertainty and energy market volatility stemmed from Middle Eastern tensions and the broader geopolitical realignment between Western nations, China, and Russia.

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture was experiencing acute polarization and anxiety in October 2023, with international conflict, domestic political dysfunction, and economic uncertainty creating a sense of instability. The Broncos were in a disappointing 2023 NFL season (they would finish 5-12), dampening sports optimism. Colorado's cultural institutions were in their fall programming season. The broader zeitgeist reflected concern about global unrest, domestic division, and questions about leadership and moral direction—themes that aligned naturally with the lectionary's emphasis on faith amid hardship and the spiritual danger of material attachment.
